behaviors.description: Conversation history is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when history exceeds context window.
behaviors.justification: This is a deterministic transformation at a single function/object boundary (input history -> trimmed history). A pure in-process test can reproduce real failures like off-by-one trimming, wrong ordering, or trimming the newest message. No OS/network/subprocess/LLM behavior is required.
behaviors.test_description: Call the history-preparation function with 11+ ordered messages and assert only the latest 10 (in correct order) are passed onward; include edge cases at 10 and 11 to catch boundary bugs.

behaviors.description: Generated response addresses the customer’s question instead of being generic or off-topic.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output quality and semantic relevance, which is non-deterministic and cannot be faithfully validated with mocks. A mocked model could always return on-topic text and hide real failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluation prompts against the real model with representative customer questions and score relevance (rubric/model-graded checks). Fail when responses are generic/off-topic above threshold across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check identifies a too-formal response for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: The key failure boundary is tone judgment quality (too formal vs acceptable), which depends on real model behavior. Mocking the tone checker would likely hide misclassification failures. Regeneration trigger wiring can be asserted in the same test once real misclassification is observed.
behaviors.test_description: Provide casual-tier scenarios that elicit formal drafts, run the real tone-check step, and assert it flags formality and causes a regeneration call; repeat to account for model variance.

behaviors.description: Full exchange (user message, initial response, regenerations) is persisted with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Real failure risk is in module wiring and DB persistence semantics (schema mapping, multi-record write, timestamp assignment/order). In-process mocks can miss these. No external OS/process/real network dependency is inherently required if using a real local DB.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a conversation flow against a real test DB and assert all exchange records exist (including rejected drafts/regenerations), foreign keys/session linkage are correct, and timestamps are populated and monotonic.

behaviors.description: If tone-triggered regeneration occurs, only the second response is shown to the customer; rejected draft is never exposed.
behaviors.justification: This is an orchestration/contract behavior across generator, tone-check gate, and response delivery path. A single unit test can miss leakage through real module boundaries; however, no real LLM quality or external system is strictly required if outputs are controlled.
behaviors.test_description: Drive the chat handler with controlled first/second model outputs and forced tone-trigger path, then assert the client-visible payload/stream contains only the regenerated response while the first draft remains internal-only.
